info.title: Authress
info.description:
Introduction

Welcome to the Authress Authorization API.
The Authress REST API provides the operations and resources necessary to create records, assign permissions, and verify any user in your platform.

  • Manage multitenant platforms and create user tenants for SSO connections.
  • Create records to assign roles and resources to grant access for users.
  • Check user access control by calling the authorization API at the right time.
  • Configure service clients to securely access services in your platform.

For more in-depth scenarios check out the Authress knowledge base.

get.description: Returns a paginated user list for the account. The data returned by this endpoint is highly variable based on the source OAuth provider. Avoid depending on undocumented properties as these are subject to change and can be coupled to population directly by the user's login connection.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max number of results to return

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Continuation cursor for paging

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Filter to search users by. This performs an optimized search for matching on properties in the user. The results may change over time based on improvements in the API.

parameters.name: tenantId
parameters.description: Return only users that are part of the specified tenant. Users can only be part of one tenant, using this parameter will limit returned users that have logged into this tenant.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to fetch users for the specified, but has other account permissions

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The user identifier.

get.description: Get the user data associated with a user. The data returned by this endpoint is highly variable based on the source OAuth provider. Avoid depending on undocumented properties.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to get user data.

404.description: Not found. The user does not exist.

delete.description: Removes the user, all access the user has been granted through Authress access records, and any related user data. This action is completed asynchronously.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to delete the users.

404.description: Not found. The user does not exist.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The user identifier.

put.description: Sets the user authentication configuration. This endpoint contains properties that allow detailed control over tokens generated for the user.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to update user token configuration for this user.

404.description: Not found. The user does not exist.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The user identifier.

post.description: Adds an additional identity to an existing user. This allows a user to login using multiple identity providers and SSO connections. The user will be able to login using any of the linked identities and access will be unified across all identities. The userId of the user is not changed when linking additional identities.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to link user identities.

404.description: Not found. The user does not exist.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The user identifier.

parameters.name: identityId
parameters.description: The connection ID or Connection User ID to unlink from this user.

delete.description: Removes a linked identity from a user. The user will no longer be able to login using the removed identity. If the user only has one linked identity, this operation will fail as users must have at least one identity.

204.description: Success. User identity has been removed from the user.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to update user identities.

404.description: Not found. The user does not exist.

get.description: Performs the user authorization check. Does the user have the specified permission to the resource?

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The user identifier to check and verify the permissions of.

parameters.name: resourceUri
parameters.description: The uri path of a resource to validate, must be URL encoded, uri segments are allowed, the resource must be a full path.

parameters.name: permission
parameters.description: Permission to check, '✶' and scoped permissions can also be checked here.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The requestor of the authorization check doesn't have the required permission to check the user's authorization.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have any permissions to the resource including the one requested.

get.description: Get a summary of the permissions a user has to a particular resource. For real time authorization checks, specify the permission.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The user identifier for the user to check permissions.

parameters.name: resourceUri
parameters.description: The uri path of a resource to validate, must be URL encoded, uri segments are allowed.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have any permissions to the resource.

get.description: Get a summary of the roles a user has to a particular resource. Users can be assigned roles from multiple access records, this may cause the same role to appear in the list more than once. Only contains explicitly roles assigned to the user for the specified resources. Roles granted to the user for the resource due to a parent resource assignment are not returned. For real time authorization checks, use the  /permission endpoint and specify the explicit permission.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The user to get roles for.

parameters.name: resourceUri
parameters.description: The uri path of a resource to get roles for, must be URL encoded. Checks for explicit resource roles, roles attached to parent resources are not returned.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have any roles to the resource.

get.description: Get the users resources. This result is a list of resource uris that a user has an permission to. By default only the top level matching resources are returned. To get a user's list of deeply nested resources, set the `collectionConfiguration` to be `INCLUDE_NESTED`. This collection is paginated.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The user identifier for which to list all accessible resources.

parameters.name: resourceUri
parameters.description: The top level uri path of a resource to query for. Will only match explicit or nested sub-resources. Will not partial match resource names.

parameters.name: collectionConfiguration
parameters.description:
`TOP_LEVEL_ONLY` - returns only directly nested resources under the resourceUri. A query to `resourceUri=Collection` will return `Collection/resource_1`.
`INCLUDE_NESTED` - will return all sub-resources as well as deeply nested resources that the user has the specified permission to. A query to `resourceUri=Collection` will return `Collection/namespaces/ns/resources/resource_1`.

To return matching resources for nested resources, set this parameter to `INCLUDE_NESTED`.

parameters.name: permissions
parameters.description: Permission to check, '✶' and scoped permissions can also be checked here. By default if the user has any permission explicitly to a resource, it will be included in the list.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max number of results to return

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Continuation cursor for paging

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

get.description: Returns a paginated groups list for the account. Only groups the user has access to are returned. The results sort order is not stable, on subsequent requests different results may be returned. This query resource is meant for administrative actions only, therefore has a lower rate limit tier than user permissions related resources. Additionally, the results from a query to Groups may be delayed up to 5 minutes.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max number of results to return

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Continuation cursor for paging

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Filter to search groups by. This performs an optimized search for matching on properties in the group. The results may change over time based on improvements in the API.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to fetch groups, but has other account permissions

post.description: Specify users to be included in a new group. (Groups have a maximum size of 100 users)

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to create groups.

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: The identifier of the group.

get.description: A group contains multiple users which can be added to an access record, and should be assigned the same roles at the same time.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to the group, but they have other permissions to the same account.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have any permissions to the group or this group does not exist.

put.description: Updates a group adding or removing user. Change a group updates the permissions and roles the users have access to. (Groups have a maximum size of ~100KB)

parameters.name: If-Unmodified-Since
parameters.description: The expected last time the group was modified.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to update the group.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have any permissions to the group.

412.description: Precondition failed. Usually the result of a concurrent update to the group. Get the latest version and retry again.

delete.description: Remove a group, users will lose any role that was assigned through membership of this group. This action cannot be undone.

204.description: Success. The group has been deleted

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to delete the group.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have any permissions to the resource or the group no longer exists.

get.description: Permissions can be set globally at a resource level. This endpoint returns a list of resources with globally set resource permissions.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

parameters.name: resourceUri
parameters.description: The uri path of a resource to validate, must be URL encoded, uri segments are allowed.

get.description: Permissions can be set globally at a resource level. This will apply to all users in an account.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have permission to the resource.

put.description: Updates the global permissions on a resource. This applies to all users in an account.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to the resource, but they have other permissions to the same resource.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have permission to the resource.

get.description: Get the resource users with explicit access to the resource. This result is a list of users that have some permission to the resource. Users with access to parent resources and users with access only to a sub-resource will not be returned in this result. In the case that the resource has multiple users, the list will be paginated.

parameters.name: resourceUri
parameters.description: The uri path of a resource to validate, must be URL encoded, uri segments are allowed.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max number of results to return

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Continuation cursor for paging

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

get.description: Returns a paginated records list for the account. Only records the user has access to are returned. The results sort order is not stable, on subsequent requests different results may be returned. This query resource is meant for administrative actions only, therefore has a lower rate limit tier than user permissions related resources. Additionally, the results from a query to Access Records may be delayed up to 5 minutes.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max number of results to return

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Continuation cursor for paging

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Filter to search records by. This performs an optimized search for matching on properties in the record. The results may change over time based on improvements in the API.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter records by their current status.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to fetch account records, but has other account permissions.

post.description: Specify user roles for specific resources. (Records have a maximum size of ~100KB)

202.description: Success. Access record created and permissions will be propagated asynchronously.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to create records.

409.description: AccessRecordAlreadyExists. There already exists an access record with this recordId.

413.description: The size of the record is larger than allowed. Recommended action is to create another record and retry the updates.

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: The identifier of the access record.

get.description: Access records contain information assigning permissions to users for resources.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to the access record, but they have other permissions to the same account.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have any permissions to the access record or this access record does not exist.

put.description: Updates an access record adding or removing user permissions to resources. (Records have a maximum size of ~100KB)

parameters.name: If-Unmodified-Since
parameters.description: The expected last time the record was modified.

202.description: Success. Access record update request was accepted.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to update the access record.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have any permissions to the access record.

412.description: Precondition failed. Usually the result of a concurrent update to the access record. Get the latest version and retry again.

413.description: The size of the record is larger than allowed. Recommended action is to create another record and retry the updates.

delete.description: Remove an access record, removing associated permissions from all users in record. If a user has a permission from another record, that permission will not be removed. (Note: This disables the record by changing the status to DELETED but not completely remove the record for tracking purposes.

204.description: Success. The access record has been deleted

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to delete the access record.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have any permissions to the resource or the access record no longer exists.

post.description:
Invites are used to easily assign permissions to users that have not been created in your identity provider yet.
1. This generates an invite record.
2. Send the invite ID to the user.
3a. Log the user in and [pass the {inviteId} to the @authress/login SDK](https://authress.io/knowledge-base/docs/usage-guides/onboarding-users#6-account-specific-user-login--authentication)
3b. Or using the user's JWT, PATCH the /invite/{inviteId} endpoint
4. This accepts the invite.
        When the user accepts the invite they will automatically receive the permissions assigned in the Invite. Invites automatically expire after 7 days.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have the permissions to create an invite. They may have specified too many permissions in the invite.

parameters.name: inviteId
parameters.description: The identifier of the invite.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to delete the invite.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have any permissions to the invite.

patch.description:
Accepts an invite by claiming this invite by this user. The user identified by the access token in the request will gain the permissions associated with the invite.

This endpoint is unnecessary when using Authress for Login. Instead, in the Authress Login UI SDK, then pass the invite ID to the SDK, and the SDK will automatically accept the invite. Additionally, calling this endpoint will ignore any value specified in the invite defaultLoginTenantId parameter.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to update the invite.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have any permissions to the invite or the invite no longer exists because it has been used or expired.

409.description: Conflict. The invite has already been accepted.

422.description: Unprocessable Invite. The invite contains statements that should be assigned to the user, but an access record for the user already exists and is in an incompatible state with the invite statements. Use the InviteRequest parameter conflictResolutionStrategy on the CreateInvite endpoint to determine what action Authress should take in these circumstances.

get.description: Gets the invite along with the relevant invite data. Invites enable users inviting other users to their tenant, organization, or account.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to this invite, but they have other permissions to the same account.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have any permissions to the invite or the invite does not exist.

get.description: Returns a paginated request list. Only requests the user has access to are returned. This query resource is meant for administrative actions only, therefore has a lower rate limit tier than user permissions related resources.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max number of results to return

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Continuation cursor for paging

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter requests by their current status.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to fetch access requests, but has other account permissions.

post.description: Specify a request in the form of an access record that an admin can approve.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to create access requests.

422.description: Unprocessable Entity. Some of the data in the request is invalid.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description: The identifier of the access request.

get.description: Access request contain information requesting permissions for users to resources.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to the access request, but they have other permissions to the same account.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have any permissions to the access request or this access request does not exist.

patch.description: Updates an access request, approving it or denying it.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to update the access request.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have any permissions to the access request.

204.description: Success. The access request has been deleted

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to delete the access request.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have any permissions to the access request or it no longer exists.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to the role, but they have other permissions to the same account.

409.description: RoleAlreadyExists. A role with this identifier already exists

get.description: Get all the account roles. Roles contain a list of permissions that will be applied to any user or resource

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max number of results to return.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Continuation cursor for paging.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Filter to search roles by. This performs an optimized search for matching on properties in the role. The results may change over time based on improvements in the API.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to account roles, but they have other permissions to the same account.

parameters.name: roleId
parameters.description: The identifier of the role.

get.description: Roles contain a list of permissions that will be applied to any user or resource

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to the role, but they have other permissions to the same account.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have any permissions to the role or this role does not exist.

put.description: Updates a role adding or removing permissions.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to the role, but they have other permissions to the same account.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have any permissions to the role or this role does not exist.

delete.description: Remove a role. If a record references the role, that record will not be modified.

204.description: Success. The role has been deleted

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to delete the role.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have any permissions to the resource or the role no longer exists.

post.description: Claim a resource by allowing a user to pick an identifier and receive admin access to that resource if it hasn't already been claimed. This only works for resources specifically marked as CLAIM. The result will be a new access record listing that user as the admin for this resource. The resourceUri will be appended to the collection resource uri using a `/` (forward slash) automatically.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to the resource collection to claim a sub-resource.

409.description: AlreadyClaimed. The resource has already been claimed by another user or another user already has access to this resource. So admin access will not be given. The reason for this is to prevent preemptive stealing of admin access to these records.

get.description: Returns a list of accounts that the user has access to.

parameters.name: earliestCacheTime
parameters.description: Ensure the accounts list is not cached before this time.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the account

get.description: Includes the original configuration information.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have any permissions to this account or it does not exist.

post.description: An identity is a JWT subscriber *sub* and issuer *iss*. Only one account my be linked to a particular JWT combination. Allows calling the API with a federated token directly instead of using a client access key.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to update identities for the account.

get.description: Returns a list of identities linked for this account.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Not found. The user doesn't have permission to list identities for this account.

post.description: Creates a service client to interact with Authress or any other service on behalf of users. Each client has secret private keys used to authenticate with Authress. To use service clients created through other mechanisms, skip creating a client and create access records with the client identifier.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

get.description: Returns all clients that the user has access to in the account.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max number of results to return

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Continuation cursor for paging.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to the resource, but they have other permissions to the same resource.

parameters.name: clientId
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the client.

get.description: Returns all information related to client except for the private access keys.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have permissions to the client or the client does not exist.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to update the client.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have permission to the account or the client does not exist.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to delete the client.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have any permission to the client or the client does not exist

parameters.name: clientId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the client.

post.description: Create a new access key for the client or upload a compliant public key, so that a service can authenticate with Authress as that client. Using the client will allow delegation of permission checking of users. Enables machine-to-machine authentication between your services and with your end users. (There is a limit of 5 Active keys per client)

201.description: Success. Service Client access key created.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to create access keys for the client.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have any permissions to client or the client does not exist.

429.description: Too Many Requests. The client has had too many keys created in the last 24 hours.

parameters.name: clientId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the client.

parameters.name: keyId
parameters.description: The ID of the access key to remove from the client.

delete.description: Deletes an access key for a client prevent it from being used to authenticate with Authress.

204.description: Success. The access key has been deleted.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to delete access keys from a client.

404.description: Not found. The user doesn't have any permissions to the client or the client does not exist.

parameters.name: applicationId
parameters.description: The third party application, the users wants to login using their Authress User Identity. Application IDs begin with app_.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The Authress user identifier.

post.description:
Redirect the user to an external application to login them in. Use this endpoint when you want to enable a user in your platform, identified by an Authress User ID, to log into a third party external application you do not control. The user will then be logged into that platform. Authress uses OpenID and SAML configuration to securely pass the user's login session in your platform to an external platform.

For first party applications, use the Authress Login UI SDK. First party applications are those that your team, org, and company own, operate, and run (even if they are open source solutions). Third party applications are those that are operated by another company on a separate eTLD and you have no control over.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to log a user into this application.

404.description: Not found. The application or user does not exist.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Filter to search tenants by. This performs an optimized search for matching on properties in the tenant. The results may change over time based on improvements in the API.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

404.description: Not found. The extension does not extension.

delete.description: Deletes the specified extension. When deleted an extension can no longer be accessed. Additionally users cannot use that extension to log in, nor can the service client associated with the extension be used to access data secured by Authress. The related Access Records will automatically be deleted.

204.description: Success. Extension deleted. Completed disabling and deleting an extension is done asynchronously.

401.description: Unauthorized. The request JWT found in the Authorization header is no longer valid.

403.description: Forbidden. The user doesn't have permission to delete the extension.

404.description: Not found. The extension does not exist.

200.description: Success. The extension has been successfully updated

get.description:
*Note*: This endpoint is usually only used for [Authress Platform Extensions](https://authress.io/knowledge-base/docs/extensions/). If you are not building an app marketplace, then user login can be started using the relevant [Login SDK](https://authress.io/knowledge-base/docs/SDKs/authentication).

Start the OAuth login by redirecting the user to the OAuth Authorize endpoint. This generates a JWT for the user using the configured application, client ID, and connection.

The OAuth 2.1 authorization request that follows [RFC 6749](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6749). Enables users to request a JWT signed by Authress and Returns an OAuth JWT containing the relevant user claims. Tokens generated must be verified before usage by validating the `sub`, `iss`, and `aud` properties in the JWT. Please note, that the properties in the request and response use snake_case to explicitly follow the standard.

The ExtensionClient in the [@authress/login](https://github.com/Authress/authress-login.js#platform-extension-login) npm package provides all the necessary logic to make this easy.

x-code-samples.label: User Login with OAuth
Platform Extension SDK Login

parameters.name: client_id
parameters.description: For extensions, the client identifier that represents the extension. This will have a prefix `ext_`. For user login without using the Authress Login UI SDK, this `client_id` will have a prefix `app_`.

parameters.name: code_challenge
parameters.description: The PKCE Code challenge generated by the extension UI to secure the code exchange from [RFC 7636](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7636).

parameters.name: code_challenge_method
parameters.description: The method used to generate the code_challenge from the code_verifier. `code_challenge_method(code_verifier) = code_challenge`

parameters.name: response_mode
parameters.description: Store the credentials response in the specified location. Options are either 'cookie' or 'query'. Query requires the code exchange to be completed using the /api/authentication/oauth/tokens endpoint.

parameters.name: request_uri
parameters.description: An optional request_uri that points to an already existing authentication request to use to start the login flow for the user. The only currently valid option is an Authress invite identifier urn. When an invite is specified, it will be accepted and use to start the authentication session. If the invite contains a defaultLoginTenantId then that tenant configuration will be used.

parameters.name: redirect_uri
parameters.description: The location to redirect the user back to after login. This redirect_uri must be a URL that matches one of the preconfigured urls in the Authress Application.

400.description: Bad Request. There are one or more issues with the request that prevent the service from returning a valid token

401.description: Unauthorized. The credentials and temporary security token provided in the request is invalid

post.description:
*Note*: This endpoint is only to be used for [Authress Platform Extensions](https://authress.io/knowledge-base/docs/extensions/). If you are not building an app marketplace, then tokens can be directly requested for Service Clients, using the relevant [SDK](https://authress.io/app/#/api).

Request an OAuth JWT. Can either be called with service client credentials or as the second part of the user authorize login flow.
When using the `password` grant_type, service client authentication must be used via the Authress SDKs, and requires the `Authress:AuthenticateUser` role.

The OAuth 2.1 token request that follows [RFC 6749](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6749). Enables users to request a JWT signed by Authress, and returns an OAuth JWT containing the relevant user claims. Tokens generated must be verified before usage by validating the `sub`, `iss`, and `aud` properties in the JWT. Please note, that the properties in the request and response use snake_case to explicitly follow the standard.

The ExtensionClient in the [@authress/login](https://github.com/Authress/authress-login.js#platform-extension-login) npm package provides all the necessary logic to make this easy.

x-code-samples.label: OAuth SDK

200.description: Success. The credentials provided are valid and token has been created.

400.description: Bad Request. There are one or more issues with the request that prevent the service from returning a valid token

401.description: Unauthorized. The credentials and temporary security token provided in the request is invalid

PermissionedResource.description: The contents of the permission to set on the resource. Overwrites existing data.

TokenRequest.description: The contents of the permission to set on the token. Will be used instead of the users or clients full permissions. Cannot include permissions that the user or client do not have.

OAuthTokenRequest.description: The contents of an OAuth token request.

self.description: A self link pointing to this request url

applicationId.description: The unique identifier for the application. Always prefixed with app_.

name.description: The name of this application when displayed in the Authress management portal

redirectUrls.description: Specify the application host urls. When authenticating users, the redirectUrl specified in the auth request must match one of these. These support implied wildcards, and will match any additional subdomain, path, query, and hash.

audiences.description: The list of approved audiences that can be requested during authentication. Audiences requested during login will be added to the generated JWT in the `aud` claim adjacent to the user's tenants.

tokenConfiguration.description: The user token configuration, used to add attributes or claims to the generated tokens and assertions for this application.

sessionType.description: Allow users to maintain multiple sessions across devices. If disabled, users logging in on a new device deactivates sessions on all other devices.

enableAccessToToken.description: By default, Authress will return generated tokens for this application in a secure cookie. This cookie is available for use with the Authorization header. Setting enableACcessToToken to false, the application prevents access by adding the [HttpOnly](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Cookies#restrict_access_to_cookies) cookie attribute flag. When disabled, your UI will never be able to interact with the JWT access token directly which is generated by Authress. Additionally, this will result in ensureToken() returning an empty token. The Authress recommendation is to set this to true.

createdTime.description: The ISO8601 datetime when the application was created.

lastUpdated.description: The ISO8601 datetime when the application was last updated.

isActiveApplication.description: An automatically generated property that represents whether or not this application is currently is use. Applications that still significantly used cannot be deleted.

name.description: The name of this application when displayed in the Authress management portal

redirectUrls.description: Specify the application host urls. When authenticating users, the redirectUrl specified in the auth request must match one of these. These support implied wildcards, and will match any additional subdomain, path, query, and hash.

audiences.description: The list of approved audiences that can be requested during authentication. Audiences requested during login will be added to the generated JWT in the `aud` claim adjacent to the user's tenants.

tokenConfiguration.description: The user token configuration, used to add attributes or claims to the generated tokens and assertions for this application.

authenticationUrl.description: Authorization URL of the provider (where the user logs in). For OAuth this is the authorization URL. For SAML, specify the **SSO URL** in this property.

tokenUrl.description: The token exchange url (where we validate the token).

issuerUrl.description: The unique identifier tied to the provider's domain used for TLS verification. In OAuth, this is the JWT **iss** property. For SAML, specify the **Entity ID** in this property.

providerCertificate.description: The Provider's SAML public certificate which can be used to verify the signature in signed SAML assertions from the provider.

clientId.description: Provider's client ID used to verify the token

clientSecretId.description: Provider's client secret identifier used to identify the client secret within your account. Some provider require this property.

clientSecret.description: Provider's client secret used to verify the token

developerAccountId.description: The Developer Account ID associated with the credentials. This is necessary for some providers, such as Apple Login.

name.description: The name of this connection when displayed in the Authress management portal

supportedContentType.description: URL encode OAuth token parameters - Some authentication APIs don't support JSON, in these cases enable the url encoded form data parameters.

userIdExpression.description: By default, the **sub** claim of the JWT is used to identify the user from this provider. However, not all providers are OpenID compliant. Here you can provide an optional user expression to identify a new user ID from available properties found from the oidcUserEndpointUrl. (The default is **{sub}**, any claims may be used.)

trustIdentityUserId.description: Authress generates unique user IDs for every user, however if you trust your identity provider to handle unique ID generate across **ALL customers**, then it is safe to reuse the user ID from the provider.

scope.description: Default OAuth scopes to use for every request (leave blank to remove scopes).

conditions.description: Conditions are custom restrictions on connections that prevent the connection from being used when it should not be, or limits its usability for increased security.

requireBusinessAccount.description: Require the user logging in with this connect to be using a business account. When possible Authress will block user login and request that they select a different account. Enabling this may prevent some users from being able to sign up with this identity connection.

authenticationLeewayDuration.description: The maximum duration to wait between the the instant a user begins the authentication flow, to the moment login has completed. Lower values increase security by limiting attack time, longer times allow for slower connections and async login flows to complete. Authentication requests that do not complete within this duration are rejected with the Expired error. The default is 5 minutes. The minimum is one minute `PT1M` and the max is 1 hour `PT1H`. This value must conform to the ISO8601 format.

tenantConfiguration.description: Configures how tenant assignments for users are created during user authentication.

assignmentType.description:
Specify the type of tenant configuration used for assigning tenants user during login. Only connections that provide reliable email_verified identity properties can enable this setting.
  • DEFAULT - A user will only be associated with a tenant, when the tenant is specified in the loginClient.authenticate() flow.
  • TRUSTED_DOMAIN_FALLBACK - When no tenant is specified, also attempt to look up the tenant utilizing the email domain of the user from their identity provider.
  • PASSTHROUGH - When no tenant is specified, use the tenant specified by the identity provider. This will automatically create a tenant resource in Authress if one does not exist for the tenant yet.

linkingConfiguration.description: Linking configuration enables users to connect identities from different connections together, so that they can log in with either connection.

type.description:
Specify the type of linking supported by this connection. The default configuration is DISABLED, meaning that users cannot link their identity from one connection to another connection. Both connections must allow for linking for a link to be successful. By default linking is disabled because it comes with security implications, users can potentially use linking through malicious connections to steal identities from other users.
  • EXPLICIT - Users can use the linking API to link accounts.
  • AUTOMATIC - When a user signs up with a new account, if the verified email address matches an existing account, the two accounts will be automatically linked.
  • Set to null or leave unspecified to disable identity linking.

location.description: User data residency - The data residency to store the user specific data in. To ensure high performance and reliability, set to **null**, or to store the user's data only in one specific region, set the region here. Specifying the region reduces reliability, durability, and performance at the benefit of controlling the locality.

name.description: The name of the extension. This name is visible in the Authress management portal

application.description: The extension is linked to an Authress application. The application is automatically created for the extension when it is created, and the application provides the extension users the ability to log in to the platform via the application configuration.

client.description: The extension is linked to an Authress client. The Service Client is automatically created for the extension when it is created, and the permissions and roles for the extensions are connected to the client.

UserConnectionCredentials.description: The user credentials for this connection which can be used to access the connection provider APIs.

temporaryAccessToken.description: An expiring access token available to interact with the identity provider connection, if generated by the identity provider. If the identity provider requires the use of a refresh token <strong>and<strong> the Authress identity provider connection configuration includes the necessary additional connection property configuration to generate a refresh token, then Authress will automatically refresh the temporaryAccessToken.

temporaryIdToken.description: An expiring id token containing identifying information directly from the connection provider, if generated by the identity provider connection. In most cases, use the /users/{userId} endpoint instead to fetch identifying information. Note: If the identity provider requires the use of a refresh token <strong>and<strong> the Authress identity provider connection configuration includes the necessary additional connection property configuration to generate a refresh token, then Authress will automatically refresh the temporaryIdToken.

context.description: The OAuth/OpenID/SAML/Identity context that was returned from the identity provider along with the accessToken. This property will usually contain claims and JWT properties associated with the user identity at the identity provider. This is a generic object as each provider returns a non-standardized result.

authenticationUrl.description: Redirect the user to this url to automatically log them into a third-party application.

PermissionedResourceCollection.description: A collection of resource permissions that have been defined.

resourceUri.description: A resource path which can be top level, fully qualified, or end with a *. Parent resources imply permissions to sub-resources.

ResourceUsersCollection.description: A collection of users with explicit permission to a resource.

domains.description: The associated user email domains that are mapped to this tenant. When a user starts the authentication process, if they are using SSO, Authress will use their specified email address to identify which Authress Tenant to log the user in with. Only specify domains here after you have verified that the tenant owns the email domain.

tokenConfiguration.description: The Authress generated authentication token customizations for the tenant.

accessTokenDuration.description: How long should Authress generated access tokens (JWTs) last for. This controls how often tokens expiry (*exp*). The default is 24 hours. The minimum is one minute `PT1M` and the max is twenty-four hours `PT24H`. This value must conform to the ISO8601 format.

sessionDuration.description: How long should user authentication sessions last for? User authentication actions such as generating an access token will reset the timer. The default is 30 days. The minimum is one minute `PT1M` and the max is 30 days `P30D`. This value must conform to the ISO8601 format.

User.description: A user object that identifies a user.

UserIdentitySummary.description: The composite user identity stored in Authress sourced by the customer SSO/SAML/OAuth IdP, not all user related data is required in this summary.

picture.description: A url that resolves to a picture that can be rendered.

email.description: The user's verified email address sourced from their SSO IdP.

UserIdentity.description: The composite user identity stored in Authress sourced by the customer SSO/SAML/OAuth IdP.

picture.description: A url that resolves to a picture that can be rendered.

email.description: The user's verified email address sourced from their SSO IdP.

tokenConfiguration.description: The user token configuration, used to configure token properties

linkedIdentities.description: The list of the user's linked identities mapped from their identity providers selected during login.

LinkedIdentity.description: A user linked identity. The user selected this identity to login with, and may use this identity to login to their account.

connection.description: The identity related information from the linked provider.

Group.description: A group of users, which can be added to access records.

groupId.description: Unique identifier for the groupId, can be specified on record creation. Must begin with grp_.

name.description: A helpful name for this record

lastUpdated.description: The expected last time the group was updated

users.description: The list of users in this group. A group can have a maximum of 100 users.

admins.description: The list of admins that can edit this record even if they do not have global record edit permissions.

self.description: A self link pointing to this request url

groupId.description: The unique identifier for the group.

AccessRecord.description: The access record which links users to roles.

recordId.description: Unique identifier for the record, can be specified on record creation.

name.description: A helpful name for this record

capacity.description: Percentage capacity of record that is filled.

lastUpdated.description: The expected last time the record was updated

status.description: Current status of the access record.

users.description: The list of users this record applies to. The record is finite in size, for patterns where the list of users might grow indefinitely, the recommended solution is to specify each user in their own access record.

admins.description: The list of admin that can edit this record even if they do not have global record edit permissions.

groups.description: The list of groups this record applies to. Users in these groups will be receive access to the resources listed.

statements.description: A list of statements which match roles to resources.

self.description: A self link pointing to this request url

AccessRequest.description: The access requested by a user.

lastUpdated.description: The expected last time the request was updated

status.description: Current status of the access request.

self.description: A self link pointing to this request url

AccessRequestResponse.description: A dynamic body to support request PATCH operations

status.description: New result, either approve or deny the request

AccessTemplate.description: A logical grouping of access related elements

users.description: The list of users access applies to. Access templates are finite in size, for patterns where the list of users might grow indefinitely, the recommended solution is to specify each user in their own access record.

statements.description: A list of statements which match roles to resources.

Invite.description: The user invite used to invite users to your application or to Authress as an admin.

inviteId.description: The unique identifier for the invite. Use this ID to accept the invite. This parameter is ignored during invite creation.

authenticationRequestUri.description: Provided by Authress as a unique URI that can be used to start the Authress Login flow for the user. Provide this property in the `request_uri` property, when not using the Authress Login UI SDK.

linkedUserId.description: Specify a User ID the user should receive when login completes. This ID is used to automatically assign a user ID to the user rather than a dynamically generated Authress User ID when using the Authress Login UI SDK. This parameter is ignored when accepting invites directly. Note: If the user logging in has already signed up, then this parameter is ignored.

defaultLoginTenantId.description: Specify the tenant associated with the invite. This tenant ID is used to automatically select the tenant during login with Authress when using the Authress Login UI SDK. This parameter is ignored when accepting invites directly. To explicitly add a user to a tenant use the assignTenantUser API endpoint.

statements.description: A list of statements which match roles to resources. The invited user will all statements apply to them when the invite is accepted.

conflictResolutionStrategy.description:
An access record will be created when the invite is accepted. If the access record already exists, and the statements in this invite can be merged safely, then the existing record will be updated. A safe merge is one in which the current user will only gain additional access to the statements defined in the invite and other users will not gain additional access in any scenario. When this cannot be done safely, Authress will fallback to this parameter.
  • GENERATE_NEW_RECORD - (Default) Create a new access record which matches the statements in this invite. The record ID will be randomly generated and is unpredictable.
  • UNSAFE_FORCE_MERGE - Add the user and statements to the existing record. This will cause the user to gain all the permissions already defined in that record and will cause all the users currently in that record to gain all the additional permissions defined in the invite.
  • REPLACE_RECORD_DATA - Replace the existing access record users, roles, and resources with those specified in this invite.
  • SKIP_CHANGES - Do not replace, do not create, do not throw. Optimal for ensuring that all records have a known management strategy and successful invite acceptance is more important than the granted permissions.

expiryTime.description: When should the invite expire? Creating invites that are available for longer than 7 days may be a security risk as invites can be used to regain access even after a user has lost access. This is the fundamental aspect of invites, because invites exist to grant access to a user that does not currently have access. The minimum is five minutes from now, and the max is 30 days. This value must conform to the ISO8601 format. Note: Explicitly setting this parameter to a value other than the default requires the permission Invites:Create Authress:Invites

self.description: A self link pointing to this request url

users.description: The list of users this statement applies to. Users and groups can be set at either the statement level or the record level, but not both.

groups.description: The list of groups this statement applies to. Users in these groups will be receive access to the resources listed. Users and groups can be set at either the statement level or the record level, but not both.

Role.description: The role which contains a list of permissions.

roleId.description: Unique identifier for the role, can be specified on creation, and used by records to map to permissions.

name.description: A helpful name for this role

description.description: A description for when to the user as well as additional information.

collectionResource.description: The parent resource to add a sub-resource to. The resource must have a resource configuration that add the permission CLAIM for this to work.

resourceId.description: The sub-resource the user is requesting Admin ownership over.

ClientCollection.description: The collection of a list of clients

clientId.description: The unique ID of the client.

options.description: A set of client specific options

grantUserPermissionsAccess.description: Grant the client access to verify authorization on behalf of any user.

grantTokenGeneration.description: Grant the client access to generate oauth tokens on behalf of the Authress account. **Security Warning**: This means that this client can impersonate any user, and should only be used when connecting an existing custom Authorization Server to Authress, when that server does not support a standard OAuth connection.

rateLimits.description: A list of the service client rate limits. Rate Limits can be used to prevent service clients from creating too many requests to your API. They can also limit the number of requests to Authress management APIs, or contain a maximum quota for a client before it is not longer allowed to make additional requests.

verificationKeys.description: A list of the service client access keys.

clientId.description: The unique ID of the client.

rateLimits.description: A list of the service client rate limits. Rate Limits can be used to prevent service clients from creating too many requests to your API. They can also limit the number of requests to Authress management APIs, or contain a maximum quota for a client before it is not longer allowed to make additional requests.

ExtensionApplication.description: The extension's application configuration. The application contains the necessary information for users to log in to the extension.

applicationId.description: The unique ID of the application.

ClientRateLimit.description: A service client rate limit. Rate Limits can be used to prevent service clients from creating too many requests to your API. They can also limit the number of requests to Authress management APIs, or contain a maximum quota for a client before it is not longer allowed to make additional requests.

duration.description: The sliding window time slice duration for which this quota applies. This value must conform to the ISO8601 format, see the enum for allow values.

quota.description: The maximum number of requests allowed during the duration.

ClientAccessKey.description: A client access key configuration. The configuration contains information about the key. On first creation the configuration also contains the raw `clientSecret` and `accessKey` for use with OAuth and the Authress SDKs.

keyId.description: The unique ID of the client access key.

clientId.description: The unique ID of the client.

publicKey.description: Specify a public key on access key creation to bring your own private key. When left blank, Authress will automatically generate a private and public key pair and then return the private key as part of the request. This property holds the public key.

clientSecret.description: The unencoded OAuth client secret used with the OAuth endpoints to request a JWT using the `client_credentials` grant type. Pass the clientId and the clientSecret to the documented /tokens endpoint.

accessKey.description: An encoded access key which contains identifying information for client access token creation. For direct use with the Authress SDKs. This private access key must be saved on first creation as it is discarded afterwards. Authress only saves the corresponding public key to verify the private access key.

LinkUserIdentityRequest.description: Request to link an identity connection provider user ID to a specific Authress user ID.

connectionUserId.description: The user identifier in the connected identity provider.

connectionId.description: The connection ID representing the identity provider that should be linked.

UserTokenConfiguration.description: Token configuration dedicated for the specific user

defaultClaims.description: Default fallback configuration for the claims inserted into the JWTs generated by Authress.

defaultAccessTokenClaims.description: A map of claims to add to generated JWTs. These claims are used when explicitly not specified by Authress. Do not include any value specified by https://www.iana.org/assignments/jwt/jwt.xhtml as they will get overwritten. Must be smaller than 1024 Bytes.

defaultUserIdentityClaims.description: A map of claims to add to generated user identity tokens. These claims are used when explicitly not specified by Authress. Do not include any value specified by https://www.iana.org/assignments/jwt/jwt.xhtml as they will get overwritten. Must be smaller than 1024 Bytes.

IdentityRequest.description: Request to link an identity provider's audience and your app's audience with Authress.

jwt.description: A valid JWT OIDC compliant token which will still pass authentication requests to the identity provider. Must contain a unique audience and issuer.

issuer.description: The issuer of the OAuth OIDC provider's JWTs. This value should match the `iss` claim in the provided tokens exactly.

preferredAudience.description: If the `jwt` token contains more than one valid audience, then the single audience that should associated with Authress. If more than one audience is preferred, repeat this call with each one.

userIdExpression.description: By default, the **sub** claim of the JWT is used to identify the user from this provider. To use an alternate claim or a compound userId resolution, specify an expression. The resolved userId must be the same one that is later used in Authress access records.

Identity.description: The identifying information which uniquely links a JWT OIDC token to an identity provider

issuer.description: The issuer of the JWT token. This can be any OIDC compliant provider.

audience.description: The audience of the JWT token. This can be either an audience for your entire app, or one particular audience for it. If there is more than one audience, multiple identities can be created.

userIdExpression.description: By default, the **sub** claim of the JWT is used to identify the user from this provider. To use an alternate claim or a compound userId resolution, specify an expression. The resolved userId must be the same one that is later used in Authress access records.

Pagination.description: Details containing how to pagination through the collection. Consists of an optional *next* property that may contain a cursor. Pagination is mutable and the list can change between requests.

next.description: A reference to the next page in the collection, pass the cursor as a query parameter in the subsequent request to get the next page.

self.description: A self link pointing to this object's fully qualified location to retrieve the complete resource representation.
A self link pointing to this request url

next.description: A link pointing to the next page in the collection if it exists. If there is no next page this property will not exist.

Tags.description: The tags associated with this resource, this property is an map. { key1: value1, key2: value2 }

Link.description: A url linking object that complies to application/links+json RFC. Either is an IANA approved link relation or has a custom rel specified.

href.description: The absolute url pointing to the reference resource.

rel.description: Optional property indicating the type of link if it is not a default IANA approved global link relation.

PermissionObject.description: The collective action and associate grants on a permission

action.description: The action the permission grants, can be scoped using `:` and parent actions imply sub-resource permissions, action:✶ or action implies action:sub-action. This property is case-insensitive, it will always be cast to lowercase before comparing actions to user permissions.

allow.description: Does this permission grant the user the ability to execute the action?

grant.description: Allows the user to give the permission to others without being able to execute the action.

delegate.description: Allows delegating or granting the permission to others without being able to execute the action.

PermissionCollection.description: A collect of permissions that the user has to a resource.

UserRoleCollection.description: A collect of roles that the user has to a resource.

UserRole.description: A role with associated role data.

UserResourcesCollection.description: A collect of permissions that the user has to a resource.

resources.description: A list of the resources the user has some permission to.

UserToken.description: A JWT token with represents the user.

tokenId.description: The unique identifier for the token

self.description: A self link pointing to this token to be able to disable it or get its current configuration.

statements.description: A list of statements which match roles to resources. The token will have all statements apply to it.

expires.description: The ISO8601 datetime when the token will expire. Default is 24 hours from now.

OAuthTokenRequest.description: The OAuth 2.1 token request that follows [RFC 6749](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6749). The properties in the request must be snake_case to follow the standard.

client_id.description: The client identifier to constrain the token to.

client_secret.description: The secret associated with the client that authorizes the generation of token it's behalf. (Either the `client_secret` or the `code_verifier` is required)

code_verifier.description: The code verifier is the the value used in the generation of the OAuth authorization request `code_challenge` property. (Either the `client_secret` or the `code_verifier` is required)

grant_type.description: A suggestion to the token generation which type of credentials are being provided.

username.description: When using the user password grant_type, specify the username. Authress recommends this should always be an email address.

password.description: When using the user password grant_type, specify the user's password.

loginType.description: Enables additional configuration of the grant_type. In the case of user password grant_type, set this to **signup**, to enable the creation of users. Set this to **update**, force update the password associated with the user.

access_token.description: An expiring access token that can be used to access either Authress or any platform service.

code.description: The authorization code to be used with the /tokens endpoint to retrieve an access_token.

ResponseError.description: The Error Object that contains all the details related to the API request failure.

title.description: A description of the problem with this request. This property is unstable, do not use it for programmatic handling.

errorCode.description: A programmatic error code that represents this type of error, this property is stable, but non-exhaustive.

errorId.description: A unique ID that represents this request, when filing support tickets always include this value to indicate where the problem occurred.
